item.title: Coding
item.description: Minimalism In computing, minimalism refers to the application of minimalist philosophies and principles in the design and use of hardware and software. Minimalism, in this sense, means designing systems that use the least hardware and software resources possible. Wikipedia Worse is better In The Rise of Worse is Better, Gabriel claimed that “Worse-is-Better” is a model of software design and implementation which has the following characteristics (in approximately descending order of importance):
